{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Continental Chef",
  "description": "Fortafric is an Indigenous energy company, established in 2018 under Nigerian law, with its headquarters in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Abuja, Nigeria. Leveraging our in-depth knowledge of the Nigerian energy sector, we provide comprehensive energy services across the oil and gas industry as well as the power sector. Our expertise is bolstered by extensive experience gained from working with various organizations both within Nigeria and internationally.&nbsp;At Fortafric, we take pride in delivering top-notch engineering solutions, services, and expertise to the Nigerian oil, gas, and power industries. Our team of technical experts brings a wealth of experience from a diverse range of industries, ensuring that best practices are at the forefront of our service delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Continental Chef

Job Location:&nbsp;Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Continental Chef will be responsible for preparing high-quality continental dishes, maintaining excellent kitchen standards, and ensuring a consistent dining experience for staff and guests. This role requires creativity, discipline, and a strong understanding of international cuisine.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and present a variety of continental dishes to a high standard
	Develop and update menus in line with best practices and dietary needs
	Ensure proper food portioning, presentation, and hygiene standards
	Supervise kitchen operations and maintain cleanliness at all times
	Monitor food inventory, storage, and minimize wastage
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and food handling regulations
	Collaborate with management on meal planning and special requests


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an SSCE qualification with 3 years of experience.
	Proven experience as a Continental Chef or similar role
	Strong knowledge of continental cuisines.
	Culinary certification or relevant training is an added advantage
	Excellent organizational and time management skills
	Ability to work independently and maintain high standards under pressure
	Good interpersonal skills and a professional attitude


Preferred Attributes:


	Experience working in corporate or industrial kitchen settings
	Attention to detail and passion for culinary excellence
	Ability to innovate and introduce new menu ideas


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Resume to:&nbsp;hr@fortafric.com&nbsp;using &quot;Continental Chef&quot;&nbsp;as the subject of the email.",

Continental Chef Job at Fortafric Energy Limited




        
    

        
        Fortafric is an Indigenous energy company, established in 2018 under Nigerian law, with its headquarters in the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Abuja, Nigeria. Leveraging our in-depth knowledge of the Nigerian energy sector, we provide comprehensive energy services across the oil and gas industry as well as the power sector. Our expertise is bolstered by extensive experience gained from working with various organizations both within Nigeria and internationally. At Fortafric, we take pride in delivering top-notch engineering solutions, services, and expertise to the Nigerian oil, gas, and power industries. Our team of technical experts brings a wealth of experience from a diverse range of industries, ensuring that best practices are at the forefront of our service delivery.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Continental Chef

Job Location: Warri, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Continental Chef will be responsible for preparing high-quality continental dishes, maintaining excellent kitchen standards, and ensuring a consistent dining experience for staff and guests. This role requires creativity, discipline, and a strong understanding of international cuisine.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare and present a variety of continental dishes to a high standard
	Develop and update menus in line with best practices and dietary needs
	Ensure proper food portioning, presentation, and hygiene standards
	Supervise kitchen operations and maintain cleanliness at all times
	Monitor food inventory, storage, and minimize wastage
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and food handling regulations
	Collaborate with management on meal planning and special requests


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an SSCE qualification with 3 years of experience.
	Proven experience as a Continental Chef or similar role
	Strong knowledge of continental cuisines.
	Culinary certification or relevant training is an added advantage
	Excellent organizational and time management skills
	Ability to work independently and maintain high standards under pressure
	Good interpersonal skills and a professional attitude


Preferred Attributes:


	Experience working in corporate or industrial kitchen settings
	Attention to detail and passion for culinary excellence
	Ability to innovate and introduce new menu ideas


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their Resume to: [email protected] using "Continental Chef" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operation and Admin Manager",
  "description": "A4&amp;T was conceived in the year 2009 with the mindset of providing affordable Solar power solutions to the citizens of Nigeria. It was formed with a goal to replace generators even the smallest electricity generator (a.k.a &ldquo;I beta pass my neighbour&rdquo;) with Solar Power.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operation and Admin Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time
Department: Operations

Job brief


	We are seeking an experienced and organized Operations &amp; Admin Manager to coordinate Group-wide operations, logistics, procurement, fleet, and facility management functions.
	The role requires proven experience within a renewable energy company, must have a strong understanding of operational workflows, asset management, and administrative controls in the energy sector.
	The successful candidate will coordinate people, processes, and resources, support site and office operations, ensure regulatory and internal policy compliance, and contribute to the smooth delivery of renewable energy projects and services.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate daily Group operational activities across subsidiaries.
	Oversee logistics, dispatch planning, inventory flow, and supply chain activities.
	Manage fleet operations including tracking, documentation, maintenance, scheduling, and compliance.
	Supervise facility management across offices, warehouses, and project sites.
	Lead procurement activities: vendor sourcing, negotiations, and purchase compliance.
	Manage administrative operations including office systems, documentation, utilities, and security.
	Provide operational reports to the Group CEO weekly and monthly.


Key Performance Indicators (KPI&rsquo;s)


	Operational efficiency and process improvement outcomes.
	Timely completion of administrative and operational tasks.
	Budget adherence and cost control.
	Staff performance, engagement, and productivity levels.
	Compliance with internal policies and external regulations.
	Vendor performance and service delivery standards.
	Accuracy and timeliness of management reports.


Educational Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Admin, Operations Management, Supply Chain, Engineering or related field.
	MBA or additional professional certification is an added advantage.


Certification (Advantageous):


	Certified Operations Manager (COM).
	Project Management Professional (PMP) or PRINCE2.
	Chartered Institute of Administration (CIA) Certification.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strategic thinking and problem-solving.
	Attention to detail and time management.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Strong decision-making and leadership skills.
	High level of professionalism and integrity.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to:&nbsp;careers@a4tintegrated.com&nbsp;using &quot;Operations &amp; Admin Manager&quot; as the subject of the mail.
And
Click here to apply online&nbsp;

Note:&nbsp;Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.&nbsp;",

Operation and Admin Manager Job at A4&T Power Solution




        
    

        
        A4&T was conceived in the year 2009 with the mindset of providing affordable Solar power solutions to the citizens of Nigeria. It was formed with a goal to replace generators even the smallest electricity generator (a.k.a “I beta pass my neighbour”) with Solar Power.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operation and Admin Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-Time
Department: Operations

Job brief
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced and organized Operations & Admin Manager to coordinate Group-wide operations, logistics, procurement, fleet, and facility management functions.
	The role requires proven experience within a renewable energy company, must have a strong understanding of operational workflows, asset management, and administrative controls in the energy sector.
	The successful candidate will coordinate people, processes, and resources, support site and office operations, ensure regulatory and internal policy compliance, and contribute to the smooth delivery of renewable energy projects and services.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate daily Group operational activities across subsidiaries.
	Oversee logistics, dispatch planning, inventory flow, and supply chain activities.
	Manage fleet operations including tracking, documentation, maintenance, scheduling, and compliance.
	Supervise facility management across offices, warehouses, and project sites.
	Lead procurement activities: vendor sourcing, negotiations, and purchase compliance.
	Manage administrative operations including office systems, documentation, utilities, and security.
	Provide operational reports to the Group CEO weekly and monthly.


Key Performance Indicators (KPI’s)


	Operational efficiency and process improvement outcomes.
	Timely completion of administrative and operational tasks.
	Budget adherence and cost control.
	Staff performance, engagement, and productivity levels.
	Compliance with internal policies and external regulations.
	Vendor performance and service delivery standards.
	Accuracy and timeliness of management reports.


Educational Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Admin, Operations Management, Supply Chain, Engineering or related field.
	MBA or additional professional certification is an added advantage.


Certification (Advantageous):


	Certified Operations Manager (COM).
	Project Management Professional (PMP) or PRINCE2.
	Chartered Institute of Administration (CIA) Certification.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strategic thinking and problem-solving.
	Attention to detail and time management.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
	Strong decision-making and leadership skills.
	High level of professionalism and integrity.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using "Operations & Admin Manager" as the subject of the mail.
And
Click here to apply online 

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.     

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Supermarket Floor Manager Job at Kiyix Recruitment Hub
content:
KIYIX is a recruitment / HR consulting firm and our goal is to to help create one million jobs in Nigeria by 2030. At Kiyix recruitment hub, we help make recruitment a stress-free experience for both the employer and the potential job seekers.

We are a dynamic consulting firm that manages HR, recruitment, and business support services for multiple clients across various industries. We are committed to delivering professional, efficient, and people-focused solutions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Supermarket Floor Manager
Job Location: Apapa, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Supermarket Floor Manager role involves overseeing daily store operations, maintaining cleanliness, order, and safety standards, managing staff, implementing sales strategies, ensuring exceptional customer service, and maintaining inventory control.
	The manager will be tasked with ensuring proper product display, availability, and merchandising, ensure store’s efficiency, meeting sales targets, managing budgets, monitoring customer experience and resolving issues promptly. This is a full-time, on-site position located in Apapa, Lagos.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s degree/ HND in Business Administration, Retail Management, or a related field is an advantage.
	3–5 years of experience in Supermarket or retail store management with a deep understanding of daily operations.
	Strong Customer Service and Communication skills with the ability to address customer needs effectively and lead a team.
	Proficiency in Sales strategies and the ability to meet or exceed sales targets.
	Knowledge of Inventory Control practices, including stock management and ordering processes.
	Excellent leadership, problem-solving, and organisational skills.
	Proficiency in basic IT tools and retail software is a plus.
	Previous experience in managing teams and budgets is beneficial.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “ Floor Manager” as the subject of the email.

Note: For more enquires call; 07033510253.    

    

        Click here to Apply

	&lrm;Manage social media presence and create engaging content

Sales and Marketing Officer Job at Fluff and Stars




        
    

        
        At Fluff and Stars, we combine strategy, operations, and experience to help businesses and individuals achieve more. From strategic advisory and project management to brand growth, facilities management, hospitality operations, and experiential events, we bring practical expertise and thoughtful execution to everything we do. Whether you’re building a business, managing operations, hosting an event, or delivering memorable culinary experiences, we are committed to quality, clarity, and results that truly matter.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales and Marketing Officer

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop marketing campaigns for our branding and printing services (brochures, business cards, packaging, etc.)
	‎Manage social media presence and create engaging content
	‎Collaborate with designers to produce marketing collateral and promotional materials
	‎Support lead generation and business development efforts
	‎Analyze market trends and competitor activity to inform strategies
	‎Assist in event planning and trade show participation


Job Requirements


	1-3 years marketing experience, preferably in branding or print industry
	‎Strong understanding of brand strategy and print marketing
	‎Creative thinker with excellent communication skills
	‎Analytical mindset with attention to detail.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement Officer",
  "description": "WTS Energy provides recruitment and manpower services for the global oil and gas and energy industries. We supply engineers and consultants to our clients&#39; projects and operations, and perform employment outsourcing services such as workforce management in oil and gas regions around the world. WTS Energy operates globally with offices in 14 countries and is operational in over 50 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Procurement Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, RIvers

Job Description


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Procurement Officer based in Port Harcourt (PH), Rivers State, Nigeria, to manage end-to-end sourcing and supplier engagement within an upstream oil &amp; gas environment.
	The role will focus on driving cost efficiency, ensuring timely delivery of materials and services, and maintaining compliance with industry standards and company policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, evaluate, and engage suppliers for the procurement of goods and services, ensuring alignment with company standards on quality, pricing, and delivery timelines.
	Negotiate contracts, pricing, and commercial terms to secure optimal value while maintaining high-quality standards.
	Collaborate with Operations, Engineering, and Project teams to forecast procurement requirements and develop effective sourcing strategies.
	Prepare and process purchase orders with accuracy across specifications, quantities, pricing, and delivery schedules.
	Build and maintain strong supplier relationships to ensure consistent supply and resolve any issues related to delivery or product quality.
	Monitor procurement costs and implement cost-saving initiatives without compromising quality, safety, or compliance standards.
	Work closely with Inventory and Warehouse teams to maintain optimal stock levels and prevent stockouts or overstocking of critical materials.
	Conduct market intelligence and research to stay updated on pricing trends, supplier capabilities, and product innovations.
	Identify and mitigate procurement risks, including supply chain disruptions and price volatility, through contingency planning and supplier diversification.
	Maintain accurate procurement records, including purchase orders, contracts, invoices, and supplier performance data.
	Evaluate vendor performance using key metrics such as delivery timelines, quality standards, and service reliability, and recommend improvements where necessary.
	Ensure all procurement activities comply with internal policies, regulatory requirements, and HSE (Health, Safety &amp; Environmental) standards.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in procurement, preferably within the oil &amp; gas industry.
	Strong understanding of procurement processes, supplier management, and contract negotiation within the upstream sector.
	Proven negotiation skills with the ability to secure favorable commercial terms.
	High attention to detail in managing procurement documentation and contracts.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to address procurement challenges proactively.
	Proficiency in ERP/procurement systems (e.g., Oracle) and Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
	Professional certification such as CIPS is an added advantage.
	Strong ethical standards with a commitment to transparency and integrity in procurement practices.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-14",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Procurement Officer Job at WTS Energy




        
    

        
        WTS Energy provides recruitment and manpower services for the global oil and gas and energy industries. We supply engineers and consultants to our clients' projects and operations, and perform employment outsourcing services such as workforce management in oil and gas regions around the world. WTS Energy operates globally with offices in 14 countries and is operational in over 50 countries.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, RIvers

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Procurement Officer based in Port Harcourt (PH), Rivers State, Nigeria, to manage end-to-end sourcing and supplier engagement within an upstream oil & gas environment.
	The role will focus on driving cost efficiency, ensuring timely delivery of materials and services, and maintaining compliance with industry standards and company policies.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, evaluate, and engage suppliers for the procurement of goods and services, ensuring alignment with company standards on quality, pricing, and delivery timelines.
	Negotiate contracts, pricing, and commercial terms to secure optimal value while maintaining high-quality standards.
	Collaborate with Operations, Engineering, and Project teams to forecast procurement requirements and develop effective sourcing strategies.
	Prepare and process purchase orders with accuracy across specifications, quantities, pricing, and delivery schedules.
	Build and maintain strong supplier relationships to ensure consistent supply and resolve any issues related to delivery or product quality.
	Monitor procurement costs and implement cost-saving initiatives without compromising quality, safety, or compliance standards.
	Work closely with Inventory and Warehouse teams to maintain optimal stock levels and prevent stockouts or overstocking of critical materials.
	Conduct market intelligence and research to stay updated on pricing trends, supplier capabilities, and product innovations.
	Identify and mitigate procurement risks, including supply chain disruptions and price volatility, through contingency planning and supplier diversification.
	Maintain accurate procurement records, including purchase orders, contracts, invoices, and supplier performance data.
	Evaluate vendor performance using key metrics such as delivery timelines, quality standards, and service reliability, and recommend improvements where necessary.
	Ensure all procurement activities comply with internal policies, regulatory requirements, and HSE (Health, Safety & Environmental) standards.


Key Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in procurement, preferably within the oil & gas industry.
	Strong understanding of procurement processes, supplier management, and contract negotiation within the upstream sector.
	Proven negotiation skills with the ability to secure favorable commercial terms.
	High attention to detail in managing procurement documentation and contracts.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to address procurement challenges proactively.
	Proficiency in ERP/procurement systems (e.g., Oracle) and Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
	Professional certification such as CIPS is an added advantage.
	Strong ethical standards with a commitment to transparency and integrity in procurement practices.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
